The term proactive, we always use it counter opposed to reactive, obviously, as a general qualification of the type of security approach that we enable.
Versus the quality of the detection.
The detection can be fast, can be accurate, can be high fidelity, can be a whole bunch of things.

Proactive is your security posture, right?
So it's your approach to security.
Instead of waiting for the gun to be detected, you can, our software tells you that there's someone jumping a fence.
That's not a gun detection, but that's an anomalous behavior of someone that should be doing something that could lead into later on a gun detection if, you know, this person had a malicious intent.

So what we say is we give you knowledge that something out of line, anomalous behavior, is happening on your campus, right, or in your hospital, maybe in the parking lot, right, and you can go and check on or focus your attention on this particular individual so that you may stop them before they pull out the gun.
Right?

So you're going to react.
Our software will make you react faster because we can detect better.
But ideally, you want to intervene before the gun is used.

They check where they can go, how it looks like.
They prepare the bad event.
